category_sources: Official GNU Screen page describes creating and switching between terminal windows: https://www.gnu.org/software/screen/
source_facts.description says GNU Screen is a terminal multiplexer with VT100/ANSI terminal emulation.
display_name_sources: Official GNU project page uses GNU Screen branding: https://www.gnu.org/software/screen/
docs_sources: Official GNU Screen page says GNU Screen documentation can be found at the manual URL: https://www.gnu.org/software/screen/
Official Screen User's Manual is hosted on gnu.org: https://www.gnu.org/software/screen/manual/screen.html
repo_sources: Free Software Directory entry lists the VCS Repository Webview as https://git.savannah.gnu.org/cgit/screen.git/ for Screen: https://directory.fsf.org/screen.html
GNU Screen official homepage is hosted by GNU: https://www.gnu.org/software/screen/
tags_sources: Official manual documents windows, sessions, key bindings, and terminal behavior: https://www.gnu.org/software/screen/manual/screen.html
source_facts.description identifies terminal multiplexer and VT100/ANSI emulation.
